input.background: For lung transplantation, it is desirable for the donor's lungs to be of a similar size as those of the recipient. Total lung capacity (TLC) is difficult to measure, so it is useful to predict TLC from other information. The data includes the pre-transplant TLC of 32 recipients of heart-lung transplants, obtained by whole-body plethysmography, along with their age, sex, and height.
input.question: How well can an individual's lung capacity be predicted from a multiple regression model including age, sex, and height?

output.answer:
The multiple regression model is:

| Variable | Coefficient | SE    | t      | P    |
|----------|-------------|-------|--------|------|
| Constant | -6.7459     | 4.3923| -1.536 | 0.14 |
| Age      | -0.0260     | 0.0241| -1.080 | 0.29 |
| Sex      | -0.8029     | 0.5120| -1.568 | 0.13 |
| Height   | 0.0880      | 0.0252| 3.497  | 0.002|

Only height is statistically significant in this model. The residual SD is 1.185 and R² = 0.54. The residual SD represents the standard deviation of the differences between the observed values and those predicted by the regression model. This indicates a considerable reduction compared to the SD of 1.657 for the raw lung capacity values, but still suggests a large prediction error in some cases. For 5% of cases, the model is expected to err by more than about 2.371 (twice the residual SD), which is more than a third of the mean lung volume (6.05).
